info.name: Groovz API
info.description:
Groovz music intelligence platform — local dev collection.

Server: http://localhost:3001

**Testing order:**
1. Auth → Signup (tokens saved automatically)
2. Platforms → Connect Platform → copy authUrl, open in browser, complete OAuth
3. Platforms → Get Library (display_id saved automatically)
4. Playlists → Generate — Seed
5. Watch server logs for job completion (~10–30s)
6. Playlists → Get Blueprint
7. Playlists → Export to Spotify

**Auto-saved variables:** access_token · refresh_token · blueprint_id · display_id

request.description: Confirms the server is running.

Creates a new account. Tokens are saved automatically.

Region: NG | UK | EU | US | CA
Use when the access token expires (15 minutes). {{refresh_token}} is auto-populated after Signup or Login.

Returns an OAuth URL. Copy `authUrl` and open in a browser to complete OAuth. Tokens are stored automatically after the callback.

platform options: spotify | deezer | audiomack | youtube_music
Only Spotify is fully implemented in v1.

Returns liked songs and the user's playlists list. Both are fetched in parallel.

First track's `display_id` and first playlist's `display_playlist_id` are auto-saved.

Platform IDs never leave the backend — use `displayId` and `displayPlaylistId` for all subsequent calls.

response.name: 200 OK

exec: console.log('display_id updated to first playlist track: ' + res.tracks[0].displayId);

query.description: From Get Library response — auto-saved as display_playlist_id

request.description:
Fetches up to 50 tracks from a specific playlist. Run 'Get Library' first to populate `display_playlist_id`.

Updates `display_id` to the first track in the playlist — ready for seed generation.
Removes stored OAuth tokens for the platform. Replace `spotify` in the path with the target platform.
All fields optional. `diversityBias` (0.0–1.0) requires paid plan.
Queues an async generation job. Returns immediately with jobId + blueprintId. Run 'Get Library' first to populate {{display_id}}.

Text-prompt generation. HuggingFace may be slow on first call (cold start ~20s).

Optional intent overrides HuggingFace: energy (low|medium|high), tempo (slow|medium|fast), mood (string[]), durationMinutes (max 120 free tier).

response.name: 400 Duration Too Long

request.description: Combines a seed track and a text prompt. Uses both Last.fm similarity and HuggingFace intent extraction.
Fetch the generated playlist from Redis. Returns 404 if the job hasn't finished yet (wait and retry) or if the blueprint has expired (24h TTL).

request.description: Resolves each track to a real Spotify ID, creates a private playlist, and adds all matched tracks. `failedTracks` > 0 is normal.

response.name: 404 Blueprint Not Found / Expired
422 No Tracks Resolved

request.description: Returns plan, pricing for the user's region (display only), and active subscription details.

Creates a Stripe Checkout session for the user's region. Open the returned URL in a browser to complete payment.

Requires STRIPE_SECRET_KEY and STRIPE_PRICE_* env vars.

response.name: 500 Stripe Price Not Configured

request.description: Returns contract address, network, and monthly USDC amount for the frontend wallet approval flow. v2 feature — contract not yet deployed.

header.description: Auto-generated by Stripe CLI — do not send manually

request.description:
Do not send from Postman — signature check will reject it.

Test with Stripe CLI:
  stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3001/api/v1/billing/stripe/webhook
  stripe trigger checkout.session.completed

Handled events: checkout.session.completed · customer.subscription.updated · customer.subscription.deleted · invoice.payment_succeeded · invoice.payment_failed

Called by the Gelato keeper. Signature is HMAC-SHA256 of the payload body (excluding the `signature` field itself), keyed with CRYPTO_WEBHOOK_SECRET.

event options: charged | expired

Starts a new conversation. Omit `sessionId` to begin fresh.

Mistral classifies the message (off-topic → instant refusal, no HF call), then responds conversationally. If it has enough intent to generate, it triggers a playlist job and returns `blueprintId` + `jobId`.

`sessionId` is auto-saved for follow-up messages.

response.name: 200 — Mistral asks a clarifying question
200 — Mistral generates immediately
200 — Off-topic message refused

request.description:
Continues an existing conversation. Mistral sees the full history and refines the intent. Pass `{{chat_session_id}}` which was auto-saved from the first message.

If the refinement is clear enough, a new playlist is generated and the old blueprintId is replaced.

response.name: 200 — Refined and regenerated
